item.title: Oil transfer activity in Gulf of Oman slows following ship attacks, data shows
item.description:
By Jonathan Saul

LONDON, July 20 (Reuters) - Oil transfers between tankers in waters outside the Strait of Hormuz have slowed following a wave of recent attacks on vessels by Iranian forces, according to satellite analysis and sources.

Since the start of the Iran war in late February, ship-to-ship (STS) transfers between tankers have helped enable oil to leave the Gulf despite the threats to commercial vessels sailing through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Yemen's Houthis declare naval blockade against Saudi Arabia
item.description:
By Nayera Abdallah and Timour Azhari

DUBAI, July 20 (Reuters) - Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthis said on Monday they were imposing a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, a move that threatens to escalate the conflict in the region and disrupt global energy supply and trade beyond the Gulf.

The Houthis' armed forces in a statement said they were declaring "a maritime embargo against the criminal Saudi enemy, based on the equation of 'an eye for an eye', effective immediately".

item.title: US-Iran strikes: latest developments
item.description: Iran's president said his country is fighting a "full-scale war" with the United States, as Washington expanded its strikes across the Islamic republic and Tehran hit Gulf states. - Iran says in 'full-scale war' with US - Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said his country was in a "full-scale war" with the United States and that Tehran should "accept the natural consequences of this resistance".

item.title: Khalil al-Hayya: The Gaza negotiator who will now lead Hamas
item.description: For months, veteran Hamas leader Khalil al-Hayya spearheaded the group's indirect ceasefire negotiations with arch-foe Israel in an effort to halt the war in Gaza. "Khalil al-Hayya was elected by an overwhelming majority," a senior Hamas official told AFP, adding that he is expected to formally assume the role next week.

item.title: Scars of war linger on Syria's Saint Simeon monastery
item.description: Near the base of a column atop which Saint Simeon Stylites once perched, scattered stones lay bare the damage inflicted on the surrounding Byzantine monastery in northern Syria by war, looting and rampant illegal excavations. The Saint Simeon monastery itself was subjected to "looting, illegal excavation, military use and neglect", according to site director Abdel Jalil Arabi.

item.title: Who is Hamas' new leader Khalil Al-Hayya?
item.description:
(Corrects Palestinian death toll to 73,000 from 67,000, in paragraph 17)

By Nidal al-Mughrabi

July 20 (Reuters) - Khalil Al-Hayya, a senior Hamas official who survived an Israeli raid on Qatar in September last year and who is seen as having good ties with Iran, was named on Monday as the overall leader of the Palestinian militant group.

Hayya, who lost a son in the Gaza conflict and two sons in previous wars, has been based with other Hamas leaders in Doha.

item.title: Oil tops $90 as Iran warns Hormuz 'will not be safe'
item.description: Tensions escalated further after Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps vowed on Monday that not a "single drop" of oil or gas would pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Oil tops $90 as Iran warns Hormuz 'will not be safe'
item.description: Tensions escalated further after Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps vowed on Monday that not a "single drop" of oil or gas would pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Oil tops $90 as Iran warns Hormuz 'will not be safe'
item.description: Tensions escalated further after Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps vowed on Monday that not a "single drop" of oil or gas would pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Oil tops $90 as Iran warns Hormuz 'will not be safe'
item.description: Tensions escalated further after Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps vowed on Monday that not a "single drop" of oil or gas would pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Hamas names Khalil Al-Hayya as new overall leader
item.description:
By Nidal al-Mughrabi and Eman Abouhassira

CAIRO, July 20 (Reuters) - Hamas has named Khalil Al-Hayya as its overall leader, according to a statement from the Palestinian militant group on Monday, in a move an analyst says could signal a tougher stance by the group towards disarmament calls.

Hayya replaces Yahya Sinwar, who was killed in combat with Israeli forces in October 2024 as Hamas fought a full-scale, two-year war with Israel in the Gaza Strip.

item.title: Mediators propose 10-day ceasefire to revive Iran-U.S. interim deal, senior Iranian official tells Reuters
item.description:
DUBAI, July 20 (Reuters) - Mediators have passed Iran a proposal to de-escalate the war with the U.S. that would offer a 10-day ceasefire to find ways to revive an interim deal reached last month, a senior Iranian official told Reuters on Monday.

(Reporting by Dubai Newsroom; Editing by Aidan Lewis)

item.title: Bulgaria to ask parliament to allow US tanker aircraft to be stationed at military base
item.description:
SOFIA, July 20 (Reuters) - Bulgaria, a NATO and EU member, will seek parliamentary approval on Monday for up to eight U.S. tanker aircraft to be stationed at a military airport, Prime Minister Rumen Radev said, after a move to place such planes at a civilian airport triggered controversy.

The government will ask parliament to approve a request by Washington to station tanker planes at the Bezmer military facility to support the U.S. military's operations in the Middle East, Radev told an annual conference of Bulgarian Ambassadors in Sofia.

item.title: Iran says fighting 'full-scale war' with US
item.description: Iran is fighting a "full-scale war" with the United States, its president said Monday, as Washington expanded its strikes across the Islamic republic including a city hosting a nuclear power plant. "The reality is that today the Islamic Republic of Iran is engaged in a full-scale war," said President Masoud Pezeshkian, according to a presidency statement.

item.title: Cyprus marks 52 years since invasion as UN renews peace push
item.description:
(Corrects first bullet point to change "Southern" to "southern".)

NICOSIA, July 20 (Reuters) - Cyprus marked the 52-year anniversary of Turkey's 1974 invasion on Monday as the Greek and Turkish Cypriot communities prepared for a renewed effort from the United Nations to break years of deadlock in peace talks.

• Air raid sirens sounded over the southern Greek Cypriot government-controlled areas of Cyprus on Monday morning to commemorate the hour Turkish forces landed in 1974, days after a short-lived Greek-inspired coup.

item.title: Trump says new Air Force One from Qatar will get 'maxed out' upgrades
item.description:
By Trevor Hunnicutt

JOINT BASE ANDREWS, Maryland, July 19 (Reuters) - U.S. President Donald Trump said on Sunday that the Qatari-donated jet that serves as an Air Force One plane will soon be sent away for upgrades, following questions about the plane's security features.

Trump started flying aboard the jumbo jet on July 1 after accepting the plane as a gift last year, when an accelerated effort to renovate the Boeing 747 for presidential use began.

item.title: Oil price gains ease after Iran points to mediation
item.description: Oil prices hit one-month highs Monday before scaling back as Iran said diplomatic exchanges with the United States were ongoing via mediators to dial back renewed conflict in the Middle East. The drop in oil prices came despite Yemen's Iran-backed Houthi rebels on Monday announcing a maritime embargo of their foe Saudi Arabia.

item.title: Houthis impose Saudi naval blockade, opening new front in US-Iran war
item.description:
By Nayera Abdallah, Eman Abouhassira and Timour Azhari

DUBAI/RIYADH, July 20 (Reuters) - Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthis said on Monday they were imposing a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, a move that opens a new front against the United States in its war on Iran and widens the threat to global energy supplies and trade beyond the Gulf.

item.title: Israel to receive more US refuelling planes as Iran attacks intensify, Israeli officials say
item.description:
By Steven Scheer

JERUSALEM, July 19 (Reuters) - Israel is preparing to receive more U.S. refuelling aircraft, an Israeli military official said on Sunday, as attacks launched by the United States and Iran have escalated over the past week.

The U.S. and Iran have intensified strikes since an interim ceasefire deal signed a month ago crumbled, raising the possibility of a return to all-out war. Israel has not joined the latest U.S. attacks.

item.title: Trump says Republicans should add Iran to Russian bill
item.description:
July 19 (Reuters) - U.S. President Donald Trump said on Sunday that Republicans should add Iran to the Russian sanctions bill.

(Reporting by Gnaneshwar Rajan in Bengaluru)
